political.s: Via parent company ESTEE LAUDER COMPANIES: FEC: $131K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 5 executive donors.

charity.s: $35M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.21% of revenue) via Estée Lauder Charitable Foundation. Certified B Corporation since 2024 (B Impact score 84.7/200).
charity.focus: Clean water access, breast cancer research, environmental conservation

environment.s: Certified B Corporation since 2024 (B Impact score 84.7/200). Climate Neutral Certified (2022) — third-party verified measurement, offsetting, and reduction of full-business carbon footprint.

animals.s: Via parent company ESTEE LAUDER COMPANIES: Buycott — animal-testing campaigns. [buycott]

wiki.controversies:
==Controversy==
In 2009, Aveda (as a subsidiary of Estée Lauder Companies Inc.) was included by the BDS campaign as one of the "Top Ten Brands to Boycott This Christmas" because "This company’s chairman Ronald Lauder is also the chairman of the Jewish National Fund..."

In 2011 Aveda was criticised by bloggers at Park City, Utah, during the Evolution of Women in Social Media conference, also known as evo'11, for announcing their no-payment policy for bloggers reviewing their products.

Aveda was also criticized for using the brand name "Indigenous", as a denigration of indigenous peoples. They have since renamed the product line.
wiki.extract: Aveda Corporation is an American cosmetics company founded by Horst Rechelbacher, now owned by Estée Lauder Companies, and headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. Aveda manufactures skin and body care, cosmetics, perfume, hair color, and hair care products, and trains students in cosmetology, massage, and esthiology at the Aveda Institutes in Minneapolis, New York City, Des Moines, Tampa Bay, Washington, D.C., Maryland, Orlando, Denver, Winnipeg, and many other cities.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
